selected_injections.injection: CEGAR Evaluation Framework — Verification Oracle for LLM Translation Pipelines
selected_injections.description: Reframe SemRec as the first systematic VERIFICATION ORACLE for LLM-based code translation. Scale CEGAR evaluation to 200+ real C functions, test with actual LLM calls, classify bug types by LLM-repairability, and demonstrate that formal counterexample feedback significantly improves LLM translation quality compared to naive retry.
selected_injections.feasibility: High — the CEGAR loop already exists and works. We need to: (1) fix the pipeline bugs, (2) scale the benchmark with real C functions, (3) run actual LLM experiments, (4) analyze convergence and bug classification.
selected_injections.distance_from_existing: No existing tool combines formal cross-language verification with LLM repair guidance. Alive2 verifies LLVM IR transformations but doesn't guide LLMs. Kani does Rust BMC but doesn't verify translations. This is a new tool category.

selected_injections.description: Build SemRec as a proper CLI tool with a verify command and a translate-with-CEGAR command. Make the pipeline actually work end-to-end by fixing the false positive bug and eliminating hand-coded Z3. Add completeness characterization for the supported fragment.
selected_injections.feasibility: High — requires fixing existing bugs and adding a thin CLI layer.
selected_injections.distance_from_existing: Exposes a unique API shape: (C_code, Rust_code) → (verdict, counterexample, repair_hint) that no other tool provides.

rationale: The CEGAR verification oracle pivot maximally leverages the existing codebase (the CEGAR loop, pipeline, SMT encoder all exist) while creating genuine novelty: the first tool that provides formal-methods-backed counterexample guidance for LLM code translation repair. Combined with fixing the pipeline to work end-to-end and scaling the benchmark, this addresses both the 'not novel' critique (new capability) and the 'not rigorous' critique (real experiments with real results).

rejected_alternatives.categorical_unifier: Too theoretical, risks 'fancy vocabulary' without practical impact
rejected_alternatives.completeness_theorem: Useful but insufficient alone to escape 'not novel' — would be an incremental theoretical addition
